commit.message:
sec: Validate email params in EmailAttachmentSettings.of() before session storage (#1489)

* sec: validate email params in EmailAttachmentSettings.of() before session storage

Adds input validation and sanitization for 7 raw user input fields in the
EmailAttachmentSettings.of() factory method:
- senderEmail: validate email format regex
- subjectEmail: strip CR/LF (prevents SMTP header injection), max 200 chars
- bodyEmail: max 10000 chars
- emailPDFPassword: strip control chars, max 100 chars
- emailPDFPasswordClue: strip control chars, max 100 chars
- encryptedMessageEmail: max 10000 chars
- emailPatientChartOption: validate against known values (doNotAddAsNote, addFullNote)

Fixes output encoding in emailCompose.jsp:
- emailPatientChartOption hidden field: use e:forHtmlAttribute()
- emailPDFPassword input: use e:forHtmlAttribute()

Adds nosemgrep comments for false positive session read/cleanup lines in
AddEForm2Action (lines 163, 165).

Adds unit tests for all validation methods.

Fixes carlos-emr/carlos#1451


Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: restore backend .env from GitHub secret on each deploy

The _work directory is wiped on every checkout so any .env placed there
manually disappears. Fix: write the file from the BACKEND_ENV repository
secret before running docker compose.

Setup (once, in GitHub UI):
  Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret
  Name:  BACKEND_ENV
  Value: (full contents of backend/.env)

commit.message:
fix: propagate density conversions to child products

When density conversions are created for a parent product, copy them
to all child products so Grocy can resolve recipe units against actual
stock items.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: propagate density conversions to child products

Grocy does not inherit product-specific conversions from parent
products. When density conversions are created for a parent (e.g.
Vehnäjauho), also copy them to all child products (e.g. Myllyn Paras
Erikoisvehnäjauho 1 kg) so Grocy can resolve recipe units against
actual stock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(eval): add tool_definitions for Foundry tool evaluators

Include QPrisma's 17 LangGraph agent tool schemas (OpenAI function-calling
format) in the evaluation data file so that Foundry tool evaluators can
assess tool selection and usage accuracy.

Changes:
- Add tool_definitions.py with all 17 tool schemas (InjectedState params excluded)
- Update generate_eval_data.py to embed tool_definitions in each data item
- Add data_mapping to route tool_definitions to evaluator inputs
- Add builtin.tool_selection and builtin.tool_output_utilization evaluators

Tool evaluators that now receive tool_definitions:
- tool_call_accuracy (was failing: 'Tool definitions input is required')
- tool_selection (new)
- tool_output_utilization (new)
- tool_call_success (doesn't need tool_definitions, but benefits from context)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: parallel fan-out/fan-in + labeled predecessor outputs

Problems fixed:
1. resolveNextNode only returned first edge target — fan-out to parallel
   branches (e.g. 毛泽东+马斯克) was silently dropped
2. Fan-in (join) missing — 巴菲特 ran without waiting for both predecessors
3. availableInputs flat-merged all outputs (key collisions, lost source info)
4. ctx.messages truncated to last 3 — downstream nodes got incomplete history

Fixes:
- Replace resolveNextNode with resolveNextNodes returning all valid targets
- Add getNodePredecessors + isNodeReady helpers for join detection
- Add pendingParallelNodes: string[] to ExecutionContext
- completeNode: fan-out → queue extra targets; fan-in → check all preds done
  before advancing; pop pendingParallelNodes when join not yet ready
- availableInputs now { globalContext, predecessorOutputs: { nodeLabel: output } }
  so each node clearly sees which output came from which predecessor
- Remove messages.slice(-3) — pass full message history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: use PostgreSQL in production

docker-compose.prod.yml:
  - Added postgres:16-alpine service with healthcheck
  - backend depends_on db (healthy) before starting
  - DATABASE_URL injected as postgresql://noos:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/noos
  - reports moved to separate noos_reports volume
  - Volumes: noos_pgdata (postgres data) + noos_reports

backend/Dockerfile:
  - Build stage: added libpq-dev for psycopg2 compilation
  - Runtime stage: added libpq5 (PostgreSQL client runtime lib)
  - CMD now runs: alembic upgrade head && uvicorn ...
    (migrations auto-apply on every deploy before server starts)

requirements.txt:
  - Added psycopg2-binary==2.9.9

backend/.env.example:
  - Replaced DATABASE_URL=sqlite with POSTGRES_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME
  - DATABASE_URL is now injected by compose, not set manually

DEPLOYMENT.md:
  - Updated secret setup instructions (POSTGRES_PASSWORD)
  - Updated backup/restore to use pg_dump / psql

Local dev (docker-compose.yml) is unchanged — still uses SQLite.
Tests are unchanged — still use sqlite:///:memory:.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add init container to backup management pod to fix PV permissions (#4)

* Add init container to backup management pod to fix PV permissions

Storage providers like Cinder and Longhorn create PVs with 755 root:root
permissions. The backup management pod runs postgres as uid 26, which
cannot write to the backup volume. Add an init container that runs as
root to chown/chmod the backup mount before the main container starts.

Modeled after the existing postgres StatefulSet init container pattern
(postgres_data_volume_init) and upstream awx-operator PRs #1805/#1998.

Claude assisted-by

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace init container with fsGroup for backup PV permissions

Use pod-level securityContext.fsGroup: 26 instead of a privileged init
container to fix backup PVC ownership on non-OpenShift clusters. The
kubelet applies the group ownership change at the node level before any
container starts, which works correctly in PodSecurity restricted
namespaces and avoids running any container as UID 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move junction-spanning filter to translation step; peptides as TSV (#22)

* Move junction-spanning filter to translate step; peptides are now TSV not FASTA

- translate_peptides.py: extract junction-spanning 9-mers directly from contigs;
  output TSV (contig_key, start_nt, peptide) instead of 16-mer FASTA
- run_mhcflurry.py: reads peptides TSV; removed all frame/9-mer extraction logic
- translate.smk / predict.smk: wired to new TSV intermediate
- generate_report.py: uses contig_key/start_nt/peptide columns directly; fixed
  pipeline diagram text and _load_contigs docstring
- config.yaml / test_config.yaml: removed stale translation section and
  mhcflurry.peptide_length
- tests: rewrote test_translate_peptides.py for extract_spanning_9mers;
  rewrote test_run_mhcflurry.py for TSV I/O and binder classification (21 pass)
- docs/METHODS.md: updated §5 with new contig/frame visualisation and spanning
  condition table
- docs/DISCUSSIONS.md: added section on upstream_nt=26 vs 24

Closes #20

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add regression test with golden snapshot to detect unintended output changes

- test_regression.py: compares pipeline outputs (junction counts, contig
  counts, peptide counts, strong-binder peptide set) against a committed
  golden snapshot; fails if anything changes unexpectedly
- golden/test_metrics.json: initial snapshot from the issue #20 pipeline
  (1371 9-mers, 11 strong / 41 weak binders on chr22 test dataset)

The golden was first created from the main branch to confirm the difference
introduced by issue #20 (+140 peptides, +6 weak binders, same 10 strong
binders), then updated to the new expected values.

Usage:
  pytest workflow/tests/test_regression.py        # CI check
  python workflow/tests/test_regression.py        # manual compare
  python workflow/tests/test_regression.py --update-golden  # after intentional change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add .DS_Store to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Skip regression test in CI when pipeline results are absent

The regression test reads results/test/ which is gitignored and only exists
after a local pipeline run. Skip automatically via pytest.skip() so CI does
not fail on FileNotFoundError.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: switch production database to PostgreSQL

requirements.txt:
  - Add psycopg2-binary>=2.9.9

backend/Dockerfile:
  - Builder stage: add libpq-dev (needed to compile psycopg2)
  - Runtime stage: add libpq5 (psycopg2 runtime dependency)

docker-compose.prod.yml:
  - Add postgres:16-alpine service with healthcheck
  - DATABASE_URL injected as postgresql://noos:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@db:5432/noos
  - backend depends_on db (service_healthy)
  - noos_pgdata volume for PostgreSQL data
  - noos_reports volume for generated reports

backend/.env.example:
  - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=CHANGE_ME (only credential needed in .env)
  - DATABASE_URL is now injected by compose (not set in .env)
  - Documents SQLite alternative for local dev

Local dev (docker-compose.yml) is unchanged — still uses SQLite.
Tests are unchanged — still use sqlite:///:memory: + StaticPool.
Alembic migrations work with both databases unchanged.

First-time setup on Orange Pi:
  cp backend/.env.example backend/.env
  # set POSTGRES_PASSWORD, SECRET_KEY, ADMIN_PASSWORD
  docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d --build

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: harden wheel validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add Foundry evaluator-optimizer integration (experimental) (#384)

## Summary

Adds Azure Foundry agent evaluator integration for LangGraph,
implementing the [evaluator-optimizer
pattern](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/workflows-agents#evaluator-optimizer)
with real [Foundry builtin
evaluators](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/concepts/evaluation-evaluators/agent-evaluators).
All new APIs are marked `@experimental()`.

## Library: `langchain_azure_ai.evaluation` (experimental)

New evaluation module with Foundry evaluator wrappers:

- **`FoundryEvaluator`** — wraps Foundry builtin evaluators, creates
eval definitions, runs them, polls for results, and parses outputs
- **`FoundryEvaluatorSuite`** — runs multiple evaluators against the
same input, tracks last results via `all_passed`, and now exposes
`close()` to propagate cached-client cleanup
- **`messages_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain messages to
Foundry/OpenAI-style query/response payloads
- **`tool_schemas_to_foundry_format()`** — converts LangChain tool
objects to Foundry tool definitions
- **`create_eval_optimize_subgraph()`** — reusable LangGraph builder for
evaluate → conditional refine loops
- **`create_analyst_subgraph()`** — analyst-oriented helper now
parameterized via transformation callbacks instead of sample-specific
imports/state assumptions

## Tracer integration

- Adds `gen_ai.evaluation.*` semantic convention constants to the tracer
`Attrs` surface
- Adds `emit_evaluation_event()` to `AzureAIOpenTelemetryTracer` for
`gen_ai.evaluation.result` events attached to the evaluated
`invoke_agent` span

## Review-driven refinements

- Removed sample-specific `importlib.import_module("eval_config")`
coupling from library code
- Enforced iteration limits in graph state rather than a shared closure
counter, making eval-optimize loops per-invocation and concurrency-safe
- Added reusable `state_to_eval_input` / `eval_output_to_state`
callbacks so analyst helpers can adapt caller-specific state schemas
- Hardened section/state access so dict/object-shaped inputs are handled
safely
- Cached/reused Foundry clients with locking and added `close()`
lifecycle support
- `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()` now propagates evaluator cleanup so
suite users can release cached project/OpenAI clients
- `_get_client()` now takes the client lock even when returning cached
clients, avoiding races with `close()`, and its implementation/docstring
were simplified to match the actual control flow
- Switched polling to `time.monotonic()` for accurate timeout tracking
- Timed-out eval runs now return explicit failed `FoundryEvalResult`
values with timeout explanations
- Normalized label -> passed handling consistently across response
parsing branches
- Removed duplicate unused evaluation constants from the legacy
`_semantic_conventions_gen_ai.py` module so the tracer has a single
canonical attribute definition surface

## Tests

Expanded unit coverage now validates:
- Message/tool conversion helpers
- Foundry evaluator wrappers and timeout behavior
- Client reuse behavior and locked cached-client reads
- Suite-level cleanup via `FoundryEvaluatorSuite.close()`
- Consistent result parsing across API shapes
- Tracer `emit_evaluation_event()` output
- Evaluator-optimizer helpers, concurrent iteration limiting, custom
state mapping, and safe section handling

Targeted validation currently passes (`test_evaluation.py`, ruff, mypy
on changed files).

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use temp dir for gh-pages push to avoid .gitignore pollution

The orphan branch approach had two bugs:
1. git clean -fd removed .gitignore itself (untracked file), so git add .
   committed node_modules/ and generated/ into gh-pages
2. git checkout - failed after orphan branch creation (no previous ref)

Fix: clone/init gh-pages into /tmp/gh-pages-work and copy only the
exact files needed. No branch switching, no .gitignore confusion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor get_storage_path* functions (#12906)

We were using Project.get_storage_path, which required to manually pass
attributes from a version. Most of the time we already have a version
object in place, so I "moved" the method to the Version class. The old
method was also used to get file of a downloadable media type, I moved
this to another method. Version.get_storage_path also accepts a
filename, so we don't need to join the paths manually everytime we want
to get the storage path from a file.

We also were setting a version_type attribute to force all storage calls
to be external/internal, but we should make sure the version we are
getting the file from matches the external domain it's served from, this
check is already done by the unresolver. We also have tests for this
already.

Project.get_storage_paths wasn't returning the paths used by PR
previews, we were missing deleting those from storage when the project
was deleted.

Ref https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/12899
Closes https://github.com/readthedocs/readthedocs.org/issues/10220

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: stsewd <4975310+stsewd@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix coach-player linking root cause + real-time roster + role protection (38 tests pass)

ROOT CAUSE FIX — why players were never added to coach roster:
- linkingService.js: removed playerCoach write from player's batch.
  playerCoach/{email} requires isCoach() Firestore permission.
  Player is not a coach → entire batch silently failed → roster never updated.
  Coach already writes playerCoach on sendInvite; player write was redundant.

ROSTER NOW REAL-TIME:
- CoachRosterScreen: full rewrite using onSnapshot instead of getDocs.
  Coach sees players appear instantly on the roster the moment they log in.
  Added: componentWillUnmount cleanup, cancel-invite button, linked/joined dates,
  pending invite timestamps, pull-to-refresh, empty state with instructions.
- CoachHomeScreen: switched to onSnapshot for live player count.
  playerCount now filters invited:false (active players only, not pending invites).
  newEvalCount uses player.uid (not sanitizedEmail) for correct eval queries.
  Added componentWillUnmount cleanup for listener.

ROLE PROTECTION:
- SelectProfileScreen: Coach option now requires an access code modal.
  Code verified against Firestore appConfig/settings.coachCode (fallback: BACECAMP-COACH).
  Regular players who don't know the code cannot register as coach.
- ProfileScreen: players cannot switch to coach. Coaches can preview player mode.
  isAdmin flag: users with isAdmin:true in Firestore can switch freely (for testing).
  Set isAdmin:true in Firebase console on your account to enable free role switching.
- firestore.rules: added appConfig read (isAuth) / write (isCoach) rules.
  Added workoutRatings/{uid}/ratings rule. Deployed.

TEST SUITE:
- __tests__/linking.test.js: 38 tests covering email sanitisation, roster filtering,
  evalDotColor, role switching guards, coach code verification, batch integrity
  (verifies playerCoach NOT in player batch, IS in coach invite batch). All pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove Pages deployment from release.yml (gh-pages environment blocks
  tag-triggered deployments by default — only branch refs are allowed)

  branch, then dispatches deploy-docs.yml to do the actual Pages deploy

commit.message:
fix: strip transport prefix before prepending containers-storage: (#11)

skopeoExportOCI and CheckImage both prepend their own transport prefix
(containers-storage: / docker://) to the image reference. When the
caller passes an image that already carries a containers-storage: prefix
(as the tuna-installer live-ISO path does), this produces a double prefix
that causes skopeo to fail:

  containers-storage:containers-storage:ghcr.io/org/image:tag

Add bareImageRef() which strips any OCI transport prefix
("scheme://ref" or "scheme:ref" style) before the transport is added
back by the caller. Apply it in skopeoExportOCI (line 443) and both
SkopeoInspectFn calls in CheckImage (lines 612/615).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tonybeal40.github.io
repository.description: GitHub Pages site for Tony Beal – ExecOpsPro Raleigh-Durham

commit.message:
fix(gauge): correct needle rotation by applying -90° Canvas offset

Canvas arc() measures 0° at 3 o'clock (east), but gauge conventions
expect 0° at 6 o'clock (south). Apply a -90° angleOffset to all
angle calculations (ticks, numbers, inner ring, arc bar, needle,
conic gradient) so the gauge spans 7 o'clock → 5 o'clock as expected.

Sub-dial angles (hardcoded R(-225)) are unaffected.

Fixes #43

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: block image/images from being overwritten in user_update

The getImageAttribute accessor converts relative paths to full URLs.
When Flutter sends the user data back via user_update, the full URL
overwrites the relative path in the DB, breaking file_exists checks.
Images should only be updated via upload_images endpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GUI trade_type keys to match registry strings stored in DB

trade_type keys in the DB/REST API are the registry strings, not class names:
  OptionTrade        → 'Option'
  EquityOptionTrade  → 'EquityOption'
  CreditDefaultSwap  → 'CDS'
  InterestRateSwaption → 'IRSwaption'

- Fix INST_ICONS dict keys to match registry strings
- Fix all elif inst == branches in render_detail() to match registry strings
- REST API /trades/{id}/price now works for all 10 trade types
- FastAPI backend started on port 8000 (uvicorn)

Release v0.20.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(coordinator): debounce sync_status car_off transition by 30 s

Prevents rapid live→car_off→live oscillation in the HA sync_status sensor
that occurred whenever the ECU briefly stopped responding between OBD poll
cycles (~20 s cycles caused by MAX_CONSECUTIVE_OBD_TIMEOUTS = 20).

Changes:
- const.py: add SYNC_CAR_OFF_DEBOUNCE_S = 30
- coordinator.py: add _car_off_since_mono monotonic timestamp; add
  _update_car_off_debounce() method called on each successful health poll;
  rewrite sync_status property to require the car-off condition to persist
  for >= 30 s before returning SYNC_STATUS_CAR_OFF.  A live reading resets
  the timer immediately.
- tests: import SYNC_CAR_OFF_DEBOUNCE_S; update 4 existing car-off tests
  to pre-set _car_off_since_mono (debounce elapsed); add 3 new tests
  covering: debounce window (stays live), reset on live data, and timer
  management via _update_car_off_debounce().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
